response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and systems software engineer with over 15 years of professional experience, but his background is almost entirely in web development, GIS platforms, mobile apps, and developer tooling. The Principal Performance Architect role demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware optimization, deep-learning workload analysis, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear in his resume. His closest relevant work is low-latency microservices at Sociomantic and C/C++ component development at National Instruments, but neither approaches the hardware-level AI infrastructure focus required here.
strengths: Strong low-latency systems experience at Sociomantic (async I/O, high-performance databases, millions of HTTP requests/second) shows genuine performance engineering instinct
Breadth of languages including C/C++ and DLang, plus embedded systems exposure, indicates ability to operate closer to the metal than a typical web developer
Current role at re:cinq involves AI-agent infrastructure and MCP frameworks, suggesting recent engagement with the AI tooling space
concerns: Zero demonstrated experience with SoC/IP modeling, SIMD/MIMD architectures, GPU workloads, or any hardware-level AI accelerator optimization — the core technical requirements of this role
No background in deep neural network training or inference optimization; AI exposure is limited to agentic software tooling rather than ML performance engineering
Career trajectory is firmly in full-stack web/backend engineering with no signal of a pivot toward hardware-software co-design or computer architecture work

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP or hardware architecture experience
No deep learning workload or GPU expertise
